context.title: Designing delightful frontends with GPT-5.4 | OpenAI Developers
context.summary: Practical techniques for steering GPT-5.4 toward polished, production-ready frontend designs.
guidance: stronger image understanding throughout the design process
more functionally complete apps and websites
better use of tools to inspect, test, and verify its own work

context.title: Building frontend UIs with Codex and Figma | OpenAI Developers
context.summary: Use Codex and Figma to bring real, running interfaces into Figma, refine them, and bring changes back to Codex.
guidance: Decide to either create a new Figma file or use an existing one.
Determine which workspace to place the file in.
Set up the application for UI capture.
Open a new browser session of your application.

context.title: Run long horizon tasks with Codex | OpenAI Developers
guidance: It’s better at multi-step execution (plan → implement → validate → repair).
It’s easier to steer mid-flight without resetting the whole run (course corrections don’t wipe progress).
Plan
Edit code

context.title: Shell + Skills + Compaction: Tips for long-running agents that do real work | OpenAI Developers
context.summary: Practical patterns for building with skills, hosted shell, and server-side compaction in the Responses API.
guidance: Skills (aligned with the Agent Skills open standard): reusable, versioned instructions you can mount into containers so that agents can execute tasks more reliably.
Upgraded shell tool: an OpenAI hosted container with controlled internet access, where an agent can install dependencies, run scripts, and write outputs (for example, reports and artifacts).
Server-side compaction : an easy way to automatically compact long agentic runs so that you never hit context limits.
Hosted containers managed by OpenAI.
